verses.kjv: And he commanded the steward of his house, saying, Fill the men’s sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put every man’s money in his sack’s mouth.
verses.ct: Joseph gave orders to his household steward, saying, "Pack the men's sacks with as much food as they can carry, and place each man's money back at the top of his sack."
verses.web: He commanded the steward of his house, saying, “Fill the men’s sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man’s money in his sack’s mouth.

verses.kjv: And put my cup, the silver cup, in the sack’s mouth of the youngest, and his corn money. And he did according to the word that Joseph had spoken.
verses.ct: "Then place my cup, the silver cup, at the top of the youngest one's sack, along with his grain money." The steward did exactly as Joseph instructed.
verses.web: Put my cup, the silver cup, in the sack’s mouth of the youngest, with his grain money.” He did according to the word that Joseph had spoken.

verses.kjv: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away, they and their asses.
verses.ct: At first light the next morning, the men were sent on their way with their donkeys.
verses.web: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away, they and their donkeys.

verses.kjv: And when they were gone out of the city, and not yet far off, Joseph said unto his steward, Up, follow after the men; and when thou dost overtake them, say unto them, Wherefore have ye rewarded evil for good?
verses.ct: They had barely left the city and were not far away when Joseph told his steward, "Get up and chase after those men. When you catch up to them, ask them, 'Why have you repaid kindness with evil?'"
verses.web: When they had gone out of the city, and were not yet far off, Joseph said to his steward, “Up, follow after the men. When you overtake them, ask them, ‘Why have you rewarded evil for good?

verses.kjv: Is not this it in which my lord drinketh, and whereby indeed he divineth? ye have done evil in so doing.
verses.ct: "'Isn't this the cup that my master drinks from and uses for divination? What you have done is wicked.'"
verses.web: Isn’t this that from which my lord drinks, and by which he indeed divines? You have done evil in so doing.’ ”

verses.kjv: And he overtook them, and he spake unto them these same words.
verses.ct: The steward caught up with them and repeated these exact words to them.
verses.web: He overtook them, and he spoke these words to them.

verses.kjv: And they said unto him, Wherefore saith my lord these words? God forbid that thy servants should do according to this thing:
verses.ct: They replied, "Why would my lord say such things? May God forbid that your servants would ever do something like that!"
verses.web: They said to him, “Why does my lord speak such words as these? Far be it from your servants that they should do such a thing!

verses.kjv: Behold, the money, which we found in our sacks’ mouths, we brought again unto thee out of the land of Canaan: how then should we steal out of thy lord’s house silver or gold?
verses.ct: "Look, we even brought back the money we discovered at the tops of our sacks all the way from the land of Canaan. So why would we steal silver or gold from your master's house?"
verses.web: Behold, the money, which we found in our sacks’ mouths, we brought again to you out of the land of Canaan. How then should we steal silver or gold out of your lord’s house?

verses.kjv: With whomsoever of thy servants it be found, both let him die, and we also will be my lord’s bondmen.
verses.ct: "If it is found with any of your servants, let that person die, and the rest of us will become my lord's slaves."
verses.web: With whomever of your servants it is found, let him die, and we also will be my lord’s slaves.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Now also let it be according unto your words: he with whom it is found shall be my servant; and ye shall be blameless.
verses.ct: He answered, "Very well, let it be as you say — but only the one who has it will become my slave. The rest of you will be free of blame."
verses.web: He said, “Now also let it be according to your words. He with whom it is found will be my slave; and you will be blameless.”

verses.kjv: Then they speedily took down every man his sack to the ground, and opened every man his sack.
verses.ct: Each of them quickly lowered his sack to the ground and opened it.
verses.web: Then they hurried, and each man took his sack down to the ground, and each man opened his sack.

verses.kjv: And he searched, and began at the eldest, and left at the youngest: and the cup was found in Benjamin’s sack.
verses.ct: The steward searched them, starting with the oldest and finishing with the youngest, and the cup was discovered in Benjamin's sack.
verses.web: He searched, beginning with the oldest, and ending at the youngest. The cup was found in Benjamin’s sack.

verses.kjv: Then they rent their clothes, and laded every man his ass, and returned to the city.
verses.ct: They tore their clothes in grief, loaded their donkeys again, and went back to the city.
verses.web: Then they tore their clothes, and each man loaded his donkey, and returned to the city.

verses.kjv: And Judah and his brethren came to Joseph’s house; for he was yet there: and they fell before him on the ground.
verses.ct: Judah and his brothers arrived at Joseph's house while he was still there, and they threw themselves face down on the ground before him.
verses.web: Judah and his brothers came to Joseph’s house, and he was still there. They fell on the ground before him.

verses.kjv: And Joseph said unto them, What deed is this that ye have done? wot ye not that such a man as I can certainly divine?
verses.ct: Joseph asked them, "What is this you have done? Didn't you realize that a man like me can discover things through divination?"
verses.web: Joseph said to them, “What deed is this that you have done? Don’t you know that such a man as I can indeed do divination?”

verses.kjv: And Judah said, What shall we say unto my lord? what shall we speak? or how shall we clear ourselves? God hath found out the iniquity of thy servants: behold, we are my lord’s servants, both we, and he also with whom the cup is found.
verses.ct: Judah responded, "What can we say to my lord? How can we speak? How can we prove our innocence? God has uncovered the sin of your servants. Here we are — all of us are my lord's slaves, both we and the one in whose sack the cup was found."
verses.web: Judah said, “What will we tell my lord? What will we speak? How will we clear ourselves? God has found out the iniquity of your servants. Behold, we are my lord’s slaves, both we and he also in whose hand the cup is found.”

verses.kjv: And he said, God forbid that I should do so: but the man in whose hand the cup is found, he shall be my servant; and as for you, get you up in peace unto your father.
verses.ct: But Joseph said, "May God forbid that I would do such a thing! Only the man in whose possession the cup was found will be my slave. The rest of you may go back to your father in peace."
verses.web: He said, “Far be it from me that I should do so. The man in whose hand the cup is found, he will be my slave; but as for you, go up in peace to your father.”

verses.kjv: Then Judah came near unto him, and said, Oh my lord, let thy servant, I pray thee, speak a word in my lord’s ears, and let not thine anger burn against thy servant: for thou art even as Pharaoh.
verses.ct: Then Judah stepped closer to him and said, "Please, my lord, allow your servant to speak privately to you, and do not let your anger burn against your servant, for you are equal to Pharaoh himself."
verses.web: Then Judah came near to him, and said, “Oh, my lord, please let your servant speak a word in my lord’s ears, and don’t let your anger burn against your servant; for you are even as Pharaoh.

verses.kjv: My lord asked his servants, saying, Have ye a father, or a brother?
verses.ct: "My lord asked his servants, 'Do you have a father or a brother?'"
verses.web: My lord asked his servants, saying, ‘Have you a father, or a brother?’

verses.kjv: And we said unto my lord, We have a father, an old man, and a child of his old age, a little one; and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother, and his father loveth him.
verses.ct: "We answered my lord, 'We have an elderly father and a young brother born to him in his old age. That boy's brother is dead, and he is the only surviving son of his mother. His father loves him deeply.'"
verses.web: We said to my lord, ‘We have a father, an old man, and a child of his old age, a little one; and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother; and his father loves him.’

verses.kjv: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Bring him down unto me, that I may set mine eyes upon him.
verses.ct: "Then you told your servants, 'Bring him down to me so that I can see him for myself.'"
verses.web: You said to your servants, ‘Bring him down to me, that I may set my eyes on him.’

verses.kjv: And we said unto my lord, The lad cannot leave his father: for if he should leave his father, his father would die.
verses.ct: "We said to my lord, 'The boy cannot leave his father, because if he did, his father would die.'"
verses.web: We said to my lord, ‘The boy can’t leave his father, for if he should leave his father, his father would die.’

verses.kjv: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Except your youngest brother come down with you, ye shall see my face no more.
verses.ct: "But you told your servants, 'Unless your youngest brother comes with you, you will never be allowed to see me again.'"
verses.web: You said to your servants, ‘Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you will see my face no more.’

verses.kjv: And it came to pass when we came up unto thy servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.
verses.ct: "When we returned to your servant our father, we repeated to him everything my lord had said."
verses.web: When we came up to your servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.

verses.kjv: And our father said, Go again, and buy us a little food.
verses.ct: "Later our father said, 'Go back and purchase a small amount of food for us.'"
verses.web: Our father said, ‘Go again and buy us a little food.’

verses.kjv: And we said, We cannot go down: if our youngest brother be with us, then will we go down: for we may not see the man’s face, except our youngest brother be with us.
verses.ct: "We told him, 'We cannot go. We will only go if our youngest brother comes with us, because we are not permitted to see the man unless our youngest brother is present.'"
verses.web: We said, ‘We can’t go down. If our youngest brother is with us, then we will go down: for we may not see the man’s face, unless our youngest brother is with us.’

verses.kjv: And thy servant my father said unto us, Ye know that my wife bare me two sons:
verses.ct: "Your servant my father said to us, 'You know that my wife bore me two sons.'"
verses.web: Your servant, my father, said to us, ‘You know that my wife bore me two sons.

verses.kjv: And the one went out from me, and I said, Surely he is torn in pieces; and I saw him not since:
verses.ct: "'One of them left me, and I concluded that he must have been torn apart by a wild animal. I have not seen him since.'"
verses.web: One went out from me, and I said, “Surely he is torn in pieces;” and I haven’t seen him since.

verses.kjv: And if ye take this also from me, and mischief befall him, ye shall bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.
verses.ct: "'If you take this one from me too, and something terrible happens to him, you will send my gray head down to the grave in misery.'"
verses.web: If you take this one also from me, and harm happens to him, you will bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to Sheol.’

verses.kjv: Now therefore when I come to thy servant my father, and the lad be not with us; seeing that his life is bound up in the lad’s life;
verses.ct: "So now, if I return to your servant my father without the boy — since his very life is tied to the boy's life —"
verses.web: Now therefore when I come to your servant my father, and the boy is not with us; since his life is bound up in the boy’s life;

verses.kjv: It shall come to pass, when he seeth that the lad is not with us, that he will die: and thy servants shall bring down the gray hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to the grave.
verses.ct: "when he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die. Your servants will have sent the gray head of your servant our father down to the grave in sorrow."
verses.web: it will happen, when he sees that the boy is no more, that he will die. Your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant, our father, with sorrow to Sheol.

verses.kjv: For thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father, saying, If I bring him not unto thee, then I shall bear the blame to my father for ever.
verses.ct: "Your servant personally guaranteed the boy's safety to my father, saying, 'If I do not bring him back to you, I will bear the guilt before my father for the rest of my life.'"
verses.web: For your servant became collateral for the boy to my father, saying, ‘If I don’t bring him to you, then I will bear the blame to my father forever.’

verses.kjv: Now therefore, I pray thee, let thy servant abide instead of the lad a bondman to my lord; and let the lad go up with his brethren.
verses.ct: "So please, let your servant remain here as my lord's slave in place of the boy, and let the boy return home with his brothers."
verses.web: Now therefore, please let your servant stay instead of the boy, my lord’s slave; and let the boy go up with his brothers.

verses.kjv: For how shall I go up to my father, and the lad be not with me? lest peradventure I see the evil that shall come on my father.
verses.ct: "How could I possibly go back to my father without the boy? I could not bear to witness the devastation that would come upon my father."
verses.web: For how will I go up to my father, if the boy isn’t with me?—lest I see the evil that will come on my father.”
